Container.Name: Homelable-Backend
Container.Overview:
Backend for Homelable — a self-hosted infrastructure visualization solution. Provides network scanning (nmap), a healthcheck system (ping/TCP/HTTP/SSH/Prometheus), and a REST API for the frontend.
Install this container first, then install Homelable-Frontend. Both containers must use the same custom Docker network (homelable).
Default login: admin / admin — change AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH before exposing on your network.
Container.Requires: A custom Docker network named "homelable" must exist before starting this container. Create it with: docker network create homelable

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the Homelable database (SQLite)

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Secret key for JWT token signing. Generate a random string (32+ characters).

Config.Name: AUTH_USERNAME
Config.Description: Username for the Homelable web interface.

Config.Name: AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH
Config.Description: Bcrypt hash of the admin password (default: admin). Generate with: docker exec homelable-backend python -c "from passlib.context import CryptContext; print(CryptContext(schemes=['bcrypt']).hash('yourpassword'))"

Config.Name: SQLITE_PATH
Config.Description: Path to the SQLite database file inside the container.

Config.Name: CORS_ORIGINS
Config.Description: Allowed CORS origins for the frontend. Set to the URL you use to access Homelable in your browser, e.g. ["http://192.168.1.10:3000"]. Use comma-separated entries for multiple origins.

Config.Name: SCANNER_RANGES
Config.Description: JSON array of CIDR ranges to scan for network devices.

Config.Name: STATUS_CHECKER_INTERVAL
Config.Description: How often (in seconds) to check node status.
